---
title: "MyYearInData Blog"
description: "A chronological journey through data engineering, architecture, security, and AI insights."
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
css: views.css
---

<!-- View 3: Timeline / Chronicle
     Posts arranged along a vertical timeline grouped by year.
     The green line and dot markers create a visual journey
     through the author's writing history.
     Best for: Showing evolution of thinking over time.
     Reinforces authority through visible sustained output. -->

<a class="skip-to-content" href="#main-listing">Skip to article listing</a>

::: {.view-timeline role="main" id="main-listing" aria-label="Blog post timeline"}

::: {.timeline-year}
<div class="year-marker" aria-hidden="true">2026</div>

## 2026
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2026-02-18">18 February 2026</time>

### [Branding Packs Won't Just Have PDFs: They'll Have Agent Skills](/posts/agentic-branding-skills/)

<p class="entry-desc">As AI saturates every design tool, brand guidelines become data engineering concerns. Why brand governance is the next frontier for agentic systems.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Agents</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Architecture</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Data Engineering</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2026-01-25">25 January 2026</time>

### [Agentic Decomposition: A Practical Guide to Breaking Down Problems](/posts/agentic-decomposition/)

<p class="entry-desc">Before you can diagram an agentic system, you need to decompose the problem. Practical strategies for breaking complexity into tractable, agent-sized pieces.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Agents</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Architecture</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Prompt Design</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2026-01-20">January 2026</time>

### [AIOps Journey: The Four Threat Quadrants](/posts/aiops-journey-four-threat-quadrants/)

<p class="entry-desc">Mapping the threat landscape across data, model, infrastructure, and governance dimensions. A framework for reasoning about AI system security.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Security</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Architecture</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2026-01-10">January 2026</time>

### [AIOps Journey: What Is It?](/posts/aiops-journey-what-is-it/)

<p class="entry-desc">Defining AIOps beyond the buzzword. A practitioner's view of what operational AI maturity actually means for engineering teams.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Architecture</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2026-01-05">January 2026</time>

### [Obsidian as a Second Brain for AI Agents](/posts/obsidian-second-brain-ai-agents/)

<p class="entry-desc">Using Obsidian's graph-based knowledge architecture as the foundation for agent memory, retrieval, and reasoning chains.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Agents</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Architecture</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-year}
<div class="year-marker" aria-hidden="true">2025</div>

## 2025
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2025-12-15">December 2025</time>

### [Agentic Security Risks You Should Know](/posts/agentic-security-risks/)

<p class="entry-desc">The attack surface expands dramatically when agents can act. Understanding prompt injection, tool abuse, privilege escalation, and data exfiltration vectors.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">Security</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Agents</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
::: {.entry-content}
<time class="entry-date" datetime="2025-11-01">November 2025</time>

### [AI Content Labels: Why Transparency Matters](/posts/AI-Content-Labels/)

<p class="entry-desc">Introducing a labelling system for AI-assisted content. From fully human to fully generated, readers deserve to know how content was produced.</p>

<div class="entry-tags">
  <span class="entry-tag">AI</span>
  <span class="entry-tag">Ethics</span>
</div>
:::
:::

:::
